# UI TripleD

> Production-ready UI components, blocks, and full pages available in shadcn/ui and Base UI, powered by Framer Motion. Copy-paste components — not an installable npm package.

**Last Updated:** 2026-04-12T10:43:54.601Z

- Components come in **shadcn/ui**, **Base UI**, and **Carbon** variants (the suffix in the name indicates the style system, e.g. `-shadcnui`, `-baseui`, `-carbon`)
- **Copy-paste model** — browse the docs, preview a component, then copy its code directly into your project
- Built with **React 19**, **Next.js 16**, **TypeScript**, **Tailwind CSS 4**, and **Framer Motion**
- Also offers a **Landing Builder** (drag & drop shadcn/ui blocks into landing pages), **Background Builder** (shader & Aurora backgrounds), and **Grid Generator** (Tailwind CSS grids)

## Docs

- [Component Gallery](https://ui.tripled.work/components): Browse all components, blocks, and pages with live previews
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/moumen-soliman/uitripled): Source code, issues, and discussions
- [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/moumen-soliman/uitripled/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): How to contribute to UI TripleD

## Components

### Accessible Image Slider Card

**Page:** [https://ui.tripled.work/components/image-slider-card-shadcnui](https://ui.tripled.work/components/image-slider-card-shadcnui)

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { AccessibleImageSliderCard } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/cards/image-slider-card.tsx"

export function AccessibleImageSliderCardDemo() {
  return (
    <AccessibleImageSliderCard />
  )
}
```

## Installation

<CodeTabs>

<TabsList>
  <TabsTrigger value="cli">CLI</TabsTrigger>
  <TabsTrigger value="manual">Manual</TabsTrigger>
</TabsList>
<TabsContent value="cli">

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add @uitripled/image-slider-card-shadcnui
```

</TabsContent>

<TabsContent value="manual">

<Steps>

<Step>Copy and paste the following code into your project.</Step>

<ComponentSource name="image-slider-card-shadcnui" title="@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/cards/image-slider-card.tsx" />

<Step>Update the import paths to match your project setup.</Step>

</Steps>

</TabsContent>

</CodeTabs>

## Usage

```tsx showLineNumbers
import { AccessibleImageSliderCard } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/cards/image-slider-card.tsx"
```

```tsx showLineNumbers
<AccessibleImageSliderCard />
```

## Component Details

- **Category**: cards


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { AccessibleImageSliderCard } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/cards/image-slider-card.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <AccessibleImageSliderCard />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Related Components

No related components.

---

### AI Chat Interface

**Page:** [https://ui.tripled.work/components/ai-chat-interface-shadcnui](https://ui.tripled.work/components/ai-chat-interface-shadcnui)

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { AIChatInterface } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/chat/ai-chat-interface.tsx"

export function AIChatInterfaceDemo() {
  return (
    <AIChatInterface />
  )
}
```

## Installation

<CodeTabs>

<TabsList>
  <TabsTrigger value="cli">CLI</TabsTrigger>
  <TabsTrigger value="manual">Manual</TabsTrigger>
</TabsList>


</TabsContent>

</Steps>

</TabsContent>

</CodeTabs>

## Usage

```tsx showLineNumbers
import { AIChatInterface } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/chat/ai-chat-interface.tsx"
```

```tsx showLineNumbers
<AIChatInterface />
```

## Component Details

- **Category**: components


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

**UI Components**:
- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { AIChatInterface } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/chat/ai-chat-interface.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <AIChatInterface />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

### AI Glow Input

**Page:** [https://ui.tripled.work/components/ai-glow-input-shadcnui](https://ui.tripled.work/components/ai-glow-input-shadcnui)

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { AIGlowInput } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/motion-core/ai-glow-input.tsx"

export function AIGlowInputDemo() {
  return (
    <AIGlowInput />
  )
}
```

### AI Loading Skeleton

```tsx
  return (
    <AILoadingSkeleton />
  )
}
```

- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

### Animated Accordion

**Page:** [https://ui.tripled.work/components/animated-accordion-shadcnui](https://ui.tripled.work/components/animated-accordion-shadcnui)

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { AnimatedAccordion } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/disclosure/animated-accordion.tsx"

export function AnimatedAccordionDemo() {
  return (
    <AnimatedAccordion />
  )
}
```


- **Category**: components


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

**UI Components**:
- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

  return (
    <AnimatedAccordion />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

### Animated Card Stack

**Page:** [https://ui.tripled.work/components/animated-card-stack-baseui](https://ui.tripled.work/components/animated-card-stack-baseui)

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { AnimatedCardStack } from "@uitripled/react-baseui/src/components/cards/baseui/animated-card-stack-baseui.tsx"

export function AnimatedCardStackDemo() {
  return (
    <AnimatedCardStack />
  )
}
```

- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

### Animated Checkbox

**Page:** [https://ui.tripled.work/components/animated-checkbox-shadcnui](https://ui.tripled.work/components/animated-checkbox-shadcnui)

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { AnimatedCheckbox } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/forms/animated-checkbox.tsx"

export function AnimatedCheckboxDemo() {
  return (
    <AnimatedCheckbox />
  )
}
```

## Installation

<CodeTabs>

<TabsList>
  <TabsTrigger value="cli">CLI</TabsTrigger>


</TabsContent>

</CodeTabs>

## Usage

```tsx showLineNumbers
import { AnimatedCheckbox } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/forms/animated-checkbox.tsx"
```

```tsx showLineNumbers
<AnimatedCheckbox />
```

## Component Details

- **Category**: components


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

**UI Components**:
- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { AnimatedCheckbox } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/forms/animated-checkbox.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <AnimatedCheckbox />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Related Components

- [button](/docs/components/button)

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { AnimatedSidebar } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/navigation/animated-sidebar.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <AnimatedSidebar />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Related Components

- [button](/docs/components/button)

---

### Animated Tabs

**Page:** [https://ui.tripled.work/components/animated-tabs-shadcnui](https://ui.tripled.work/components/animated-tabs-shadcnui)

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { AnimatedTabs } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/tabs/animated-tabs.tsx"

export function AnimatedTabsDemo() {
  return (
    <AnimatedTabs />
  )
}
```

## Installation

<CodeTabs>

<TabsList>
  <TabsTrigger value="cli">CLI</TabsTrigger>
  <TabsTrigger value="manual">Manual</TabsTrigger>
</TabsList>
<TabsContent value="cli">

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add @uitripled/animated-tabs-shadcnui
```

</TabsContent>

<TabsContent value="manual">

<Steps>

<Step>Add the required components to your project.</Step>

The `Animated Tabs` component uses the following components. Make sure you have them installed in your project.

- button

<Step>Copy and paste the following code into your project.</Step>

<ComponentSource name="animated-tabs-shadcnui" title="@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/tabs/animated-tabs.tsx" />

<Step>Update the import paths to match your project setup.</Step>

</Steps>

</TabsContent>

</CodeTabs>

## Usage

```tsx showLineNumbers
import { AnimatedTabs } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/tabs/animated-tabs.tsx"
```

```tsx showLineNumbers
<AnimatedTabs />
```

## Component Details

- **Category**: components


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

**UI Components**:
- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { AnimatedTabs } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/tabs/animated-tabs.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <AnimatedTabs />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

- `framer-motion`
- `lucide-react`
- `react`

- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { ChatApp } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/chat/chat-app.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <ChatApp />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

- [button](/docs/components/button)

</TabsContent>

</CodeTabs>

## Usage

```tsx
import { CourseContentPage } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/course-content/course-content-page.tsx"
```

```tsx showLineNumbers

```

## Component Details

- **Category**: components


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

**UI Components**:
- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

  )
}
```

## API Reference

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Component Details

- **Category**: cards


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

**UI Components**:
- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

  return (
```
```tsx
    <EcommerceHighlightCard />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { GlassAccountSettingsCard } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/forms/glass-account-settings.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <GlassAccountSettingsCard />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

- [button](/docs/components/button)

## Usage

```tsx showLineNumbers
import { GlassSign-InCard } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/forms/glass-sign-in.tsx"
```

```tsx showLineNumbers
<GlassSign-InCard />
```

## Component Details

- **Category**: components


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

**UI Components**:
- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { GlassSign-InCard } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/forms/glass-sign-in.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <GlassSign-InCard />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Related Components

- [button](/docs/components/button)

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { HoverExpandCard } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/cards/shadcnui/hover-expand.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <HoverExpandCard />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.


</TabsContent>

<TabsContent value="manual">

<Step>Add the required components to your project.</Step>

The `Multiple Accounts Switcher` component uses the following components. Make sure you have them installed in your project.

- button

<Step>Copy and paste the following code into your project.</Step>

<ComponentSource name="multiple-accounts-shadcnui" title="@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/account-switcher/multiple-accounts.tsx" />

<Step>Update the import paths to match your project setup.</Step>

</Steps>

</TabsContent>

</CodeTabs>

## Usage

```tsx showLineNumbers
import { MultipleAccountsSwitcher } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/account-switcher/multiple-accounts.tsx"
```

```tsx showLineNumbers
<MultipleAccountsSwitcher />
```

## Component Details

- **Category**: components


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

**UI Components**:
- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { MultipleAccountsSwitcher } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/account-switcher/multiple-accounts.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <MultipleAccountsSwitcher />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

- [button](/docs/components/button)

  <TabsTrigger value="cli">CLI</TabsTrigger>
  <TabsTrigger value="manual">Manual</TabsTrigger>
</TabsList>
<TabsContent value="cli">

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add @uitripled/native-counter-up
```

</TabsContent>

<TabsContent value="manual">

<Steps>

<Step>Copy and paste the following code into your project.</Step>

<ComponentSource name="native-counter-up" title="@uitripled/react-carbon/src/components/native/native-counter-up-carbon.tsx" />

<Step>Update the import paths to match your project setup.</Step>

</Steps>

</TabsContent>

</CodeTabs>

## Usage

```tsx showLineNumbers
import { NativeCounterUp } from "@uitripled/react-carbon/src/components/native/native-counter-up-carbon.tsx"
```

```tsx showLineNumbers
<NativeCounterUp />
```

## Component Details

- **Category**: native


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { NativeCounterUp } from "@uitripled/react-carbon/src/components/native/native-counter-up-carbon.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <NativeCounterUp />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Related Components

No related components.

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

- Proper ARIA labels and roles

## Usage

```tsx
import { NativeFlipText } from "@uitripled/react-carbon/src/components/native/native-flip-text-carbon.tsx"
```

```tsx showLineNumbers
<NativeFlipText />
```

## Component Details

- **Category**: native


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { NativeFlipText } from "@uitripled/react-carbon/src/components/native/native-flip-text-carbon.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <NativeFlipText />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Related Components

No related components.

- `framer-motion`
- `react`

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management


## Component Details

- **Category**: native


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { NativeLikesCounter } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/native/native-likes-counter-shadcnui.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <NativeLikesCounter />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

No related components.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { NativeMorphingButton } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/native/native-morphing-button-shadcnui.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <NativeMorphingButton />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Related Components

No related components.

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { NativeProfileNotch } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/native/native-profile-notch-shadcnui.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <NativeProfileNotch />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"
```

```tsx
import { NativeTypewriter } from "@uitripled/react-baseui/src/components/native/native-typewriter-baseui.tsx"
```

```tsx
export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <NativeTypewriter />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

**UI Components**:
- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"
```

```tsx
import { ProfessionalResume } from "@uitripled/react-baseui/src/components/resumes/baseui/professional-resume-baseui.tsx"
```

```tsx
export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <ProfessionalResume />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Related Components

- [button](/docs/components/button)

## Usage

```tsx showLineNumbers

```

```tsx showLineNumbers

```

## Component Details

- **Category**: native


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { SocialLoginButton } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/native/social-login-button-shadcnui.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <SocialLoginButton />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { BlogBlock } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/sections/blog-block.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <BlogBlock />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

- [button](/docs/components/button)

- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Usage

```tsx showLineNumbers
import { Dashboard } from "@uitripled/react-baseui/src/components/components/stocks-dashboard/dashboard.tsx"
```

```tsx showLineNumbers
<Dashboard />
```

## Component Details

- **Category**: sections


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

**UI Components**:
- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { Dashboard } from "@uitripled/react-baseui/src/components/components/stocks-dashboard/dashboard.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <Dashboard />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Related Components

- [button](/docs/components/button)

---

### Dashboard

**Page:** [https://ui.tripled.work/components/dashboard-shadcnui](https://ui.tripled.work/components/dashboard-shadcnui)

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { Dashboard } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/components/stocks-dashboard/dashboard.tsx"

export function DashboardDemo() {
  return (
    <Dashboard />
  )
}
```

<TabsList>
  <TabsTrigger value="cli">CLI</TabsTrigger>

</TabsList>

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add @uitripled/dashboard-shadcnui
```

</TabsContent>

**Page:** [https://ui.tripled.work/components/footer-block-shadcnui](https://ui.tripled.work/components/footer-block-shadcnui)

```tsx
"use client"

export function FooterBlockDemo() {
  return (
    <FooterBlock />
  )
}
```

## Component Details

- **Category**: sections


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

**UI Components**:
- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { FooterBlock } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/sections/footer-block.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <FooterBlock />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

- **Category**: sections


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

**UI Components**:
- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Usage

```tsx showLineNumbers
import { GlassmorphismPortfolio } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/sections/glassmorphism-portfolio-block.tsx"
```

```tsx showLineNumbers
<GlassmorphismPortfolio />
```

## Component Details

- **Category**: sections


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

**UI Components**:
- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { GlassmorphismPortfolio } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/sections/glassmorphism-portfolio-block.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <GlassmorphismPortfolio />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Related Components

- [button](/docs/components/button)

---

### Glassmorphism Pricing

**Page:** [https://ui.tripled.work/components/glassmorphism-pricing-block-baseui](https://ui.tripled.work/components/glassmorphism-pricing-block-baseui)

```tsx
"use client"

export function GlassmorphismPricingDemo() {
  return (
    <GlassmorphismPricing />
  )
}
```

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { GlowyWavesHero } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/sections/glowy-waves-hero.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <GlowyWavesHero />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

- [button](/docs/components/button)

<TabsList>
  <TabsTrigger value="cli">CLI</TabsTrigger>
  <TabsTrigger value="manual">Manual</TabsTrigger>
</TabsList>
<TabsContent value="cli">

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add @uitripled/new-hero-section-shadcnui
```

</TabsContent>

<TabsContent value="manual">

<Steps>

<Step>Add the required components to your project.</Step>

The `New Hero Section` component uses the following components. Make sure you have them installed in your project.

- button

<Step>Copy and paste the following code into your project.</Step>

<ComponentSource name="new-hero-section-shadcnui" title="@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/sections/new-hero-section.tsx" />

<Step>Update the import paths to match your project setup.</Step>

</Steps>

</TabsContent>

</CodeTabs>

## Usage

```tsx showLineNumbers
import { NewHeroSection } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/sections/new-hero-section.tsx"
```

```tsx showLineNumbers
<NewHeroSection />
```

## Component Details

- **Category**: sections


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

**UI Components**:
- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { NewHeroSection } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/sections/new-hero-section.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <NewHeroSection />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Related Components

- [button](/docs/components/button)

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { ServicesGrid } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/sections/services-grid-block.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <ServicesGrid />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Related Components

- [button](/docs/components/button)

---

### Stats Counter Block

**Page:** [https://ui.tripled.work/components/stats-counter-block-baseui](https://ui.tripled.work/components/stats-counter-block-baseui)

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { StatsCounterBlock } from "@uitripled/react-baseui/src/components/sections/stats-counter-block-baseui.tsx"

export function StatsCounterBlockDemo() {
  return (
    <StatsCounterBlock />
  )
}
```

## Installation

<CodeTabs>

<TabsList>
  <TabsTrigger value="cli">CLI</TabsTrigger>
  <TabsTrigger value="manual">Manual</TabsTrigger>
</TabsList>
<TabsContent value="cli">

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add @uitripled/stats-counter-block-baseui
```

</TabsContent>

<TabsContent value="manual">

<Steps>

<Step>Add the required components to your project.</Step>

The `Stats Counter Block` component uses the following components. Make sure you have them installed in your project.

- button

<Step>Copy and paste the following code into your project.</Step>

<ComponentSource name="stats-counter-block-baseui" title="@uitripled/react-baseui/src/components/sections/stats-counter-block-baseui.tsx" />

<Step>Update the import paths to match your project setup.</Step>

</Steps>

</TabsContent>

</CodeTabs>

## Usage

```tsx showLineNumbers
import { StatsCounterBlock } from "@uitripled/react-baseui/src/components/sections/stats-counter-block-baseui.tsx"
```

```tsx showLineNumbers
<StatsCounterBlock />
```

## Component Details

- **Category**: sections


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

**UI Components**:
- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { StatsCounterBlock } from "@uitripled/react-baseui/src/components/sections/stats-counter-block-baseui.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <StatsCounterBlock />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

<Step>Update the import paths to match your project setup.</Step>

</Steps>

</TabsContent>

</CodeTabs>

## Usage

```tsx showLineNumbers
import { TimelineBlock } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/sections/timeline-block.tsx"
```

```tsx showLineNumbers
<TimelineBlock />
```

## Component Details

- **Category**: sections


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

**UI Components**:
- button

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { TimelineBlock } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/sections/timeline-block.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <TimelineBlock />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Related Components

- [button](/docs/components/button)


## Component Details

- **Category**: page


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { FeatureGridSection } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/sections/feature-grid-section.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <FeatureGridSection />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

No related components.

</Steps>

</TabsContent>

</CodeTabs>

## Usage

```tsx showLineNumbers
import { StaggeredTextHero } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/page/hero/staggered-text.tsx"
```

```tsx showLineNumbers
<StaggeredTextHero />
```

## Component Details

- **Category**: page


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { StaggeredTextHero } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/page/hero/staggered-text.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <StaggeredTextHero />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { AnimatedQuoteBlock } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/motion-core/animated-quote-block.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <AnimatedQuoteBlock />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

### Mood Gradient Button

**Page:** [https://ui.tripled.work/components/mood-gradient-button-shadcnui](https://ui.tripled.work/components/mood-gradient-button-shadcnui)

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { MoodGradientButton } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/motion-core/mood-gradient-button.tsx"

export function MoodGradientButtonDemo() {
  return (
    <MoodGradientButton />
  )
}
```

## Installation

<CodeTabs>

<TabsList>
  <TabsTrigger value="cli">CLI</TabsTrigger>
  <TabsTrigger value="manual">Manual</TabsTrigger>
</TabsList>
<TabsContent value="cli">

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add @uitripled/mood-gradient-button-shadcnui
```

</TabsContent>

<TabsContent value="manual">

<Steps>

<Step>Copy and paste the following code into your project.</Step>

<ComponentSource name="mood-gradient-button-shadcnui" title="@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/motion-core/mood-gradient-button.tsx" />

<Step>Update the import paths to match your project setup.</Step>

</Steps>

</TabsContent>

</CodeTabs>

## Usage

```tsx showLineNumbers
import { MoodGradientButton } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/motion-core/mood-gradient-button.tsx"
```

```tsx showLineNumbers
<MoodGradientButton />
```

## Component Details

- **Category**: micro


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { MoodGradientButton } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/motion-core/mood-gradient-button.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <MoodGradientButton />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Related Components

No related components.


## Component Details

- **Category**: micro


### Technical Specifications

**Dependencies**:
- `framer-motion`
- `react`

This component uses **Framer Motion** for animations and motion effects.

This component is built on top of **shadcn/ui** component primitives.

## Customization

This component can be customized by modifying the following:

- **Styling**: The component uses Tailwind CSS for styling. Customize colors, spacing, and other design tokens through Tailwind classes.
- **Props**: Pass custom props to configure the component's behavior and appearance.
- **Variants**: Create custom variants by extending the component's base styles.

## Accessibility

The component follows accessibility best practices:

- Proper ARIA labels and roles
- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- Focus management

## Examples

### Basic Example

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"

import { ShimmerButton } from "@uitripled/react-shadcn/src/components/micro/buttons/shimmer-button.tsx"

export function BasicExample() {
  return (
    <ShimmerButton />
  )
}
```

## API Reference

This component is part of the UI TripleD component library, a collection of production-ready components built with Framer Motion, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS.

## Best Practices

1. **Performance**: Consider lazy loading if used in large lists or below-the-fold content.
2. **Theming**: Ensure your theme configuration includes the necessary CSS variables.
3. **Testing**: Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.
4. **Customization**: Use props for configuration rather than modifying source code.

## Optional

- [Framer Motion Docs](https://www.framer.com/motion/): Animation library used by all components
- [shadcn/ui Docs](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs): Component primitives that shadcn variants build on
- [Base UI Docs](https://base-ui.com/): Headless component primitives that Base UI variants build on
- [Tailwind CSS v4 Docs](https://tailwindcss.com/docs): Utility-first CSS framework used for styling
